title: Neonatal Respiratory Distress
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Neonatal Respiratory Distress
Identify key diagnostic criteria for Neonatal Respiratory Distress
Recognize complications and when to escalate care

sections.title: Overview & Pathophysiology
sections.content: **Neonatal Respiratory Distress** — This section covers the fundamental pathophysiology, epidemiology, and clinical significance. Understanding the underlying mechanisms is essential for clinical reasoning and evidence-based management.

sections.title: Clinical Presentation & Diagnosis
sections.content: Key clinical features, diagnostic criteria, and workup for Neonatal Respiratory Distress. This section emphasizes pattern recognition and systematic diagnostic approaches.

sections.title: Management & Treatment
sections.content: Evidence-based management strategies for Neonatal Respiratory Distress, including first-line therapies, escalation pathways, and monitoring parameters.

sections.title: Complications & Follow-Up
sections.content: Potential complications, prognosis, and longitudinal management considerations for Neonatal Respiratory Distress.

keyTakeaways: Recognize the classic presentation of Neonatal Respiratory Distress
Identify red flags requiring urgent intervention

reviewQuestions.question: Which of the following is the MOST important initial step in evaluating a patient with suspected Neonatal Respiratory Distress?
options: Obtain a thorough history and physical examination
Consult a specialist before any evaluation
reviewQuestions.explanation: A thorough history and physical examination is always the cornerstone of clinical evaluation for Neonatal Respiratory Distress, guiding subsequent diagnostic workup and management decisions.

reviewQuestions.question: What is the MOST critical factor in determining the management approach for Neonatal Respiratory Distress?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Clinical severity and appropriate risk stratification guide management decisions in Neonatal Respiratory Distress, determining the level of intervention and monitoring required.

reviewQuestions.question: Which complication of Neonatal Respiratory Distress requires the MOST urgent intervention?
reviewQuestions.explanation: Hemodynamic instability in the setting of Neonatal Respiratory Distress requires immediate intervention including fluid resuscitation, close monitoring, and potentially escalation to critical care.

imaging_reasoning.order_reasoning: CXR is primary tool; distinguishes surgical (CDH, TEF) from medical causes
imaging_reasoning.expected_findings: RDS: diffuse ground glass; TTN: perihilar streaking; MAS: hyperinflation with patchy opacities
imaging_reasoning.management_impact: RDS → surfactant; TTN → supportive; CDH → intubate, do NOT bag-mask, surgery
